title: Malty Crunchy Malaysian Dum Dumplings
description: A malty, crunchy, crowd-pleasing Malaysian dish featuring ssamjang, perfect for any occasion, and kosher-friendly.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the sifted flour and malted milk powder.
Add the ssamjang, vegetable oil, and water to the bowl, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small balls, about 1 inch in diameter.
Roll each ball into a thin circle, then fold the circle in half to form a half-moon shape, pressing the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Heat about 2 inches of vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat until it reaches 350 degrees.
Fry the dumplings in batches until they are golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove the dumplings from the oil with a slotted spoon and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Sprinkle the fried dumplings with sesame seeds and chopped scallions.
Serve the dumplings hot with a side of ssamjang for dipping.
